title: Generated verilog can create output nets without defined nettype

features.name: output port without explicit wire in generated verilog
features.description: Code should include an output port declaration without explicit wire type, which is valid Verilog but fails when combined with default_nettype none
features.snippet: module foo(output a); assign a = 1'b0; endmodule

features.name: default_nettype none with implicit nets
features.description: Code should include a default_nettype none directive followed by code with implicitly-defined nets
features.snippet:
`default_nettype none
module bar(input clk); wire a; assign a = clk; endmodule

features.name: cross-file implicit net with default_nettype none
features.description: Code should include two files where one has output ports without wire and another has default_nettype none, causing interop failure
